user.title: Amazon Orders
user.description: Configure IMAP access for Amazon order emails. Privacy-sensitive sensor attributes can be limited here and changed later in options.

data_description.email: Email address of the mailbox that receives Amazon order emails.
data_description.imap_server: Hostname of your IMAP server, for example imap.example.com.
data_description.password: Password or app password for IMAP login.
data_description.imap_port: IMAP SSL port. The default is 993.
data_description.imap_folder: Leave blank for INBOX or enter the exact folder name, for example INBOX/Amazon.
data_description.initial_scan_days: Number of days to scan back during the first scan.
data_description.mark_as_read: Mark recognized Amazon status emails as read after processing.
data_description.require_amazon_sender: Ignore matching subjects unless sender headers use an Amazon domain.
data_description.expose_order_id: Expose Amazon order IDs in sensor attributes.
data_description.expose_item_title: Expose parsed item titles in sensor attributes.
data_description.expose_tracking_url: Expose validated HTTPS links to Amazon order or tracking pages.
data_description.expose_delivery_details: Expose delivery estimate, delivery window, delivered-at text, and item count from email content.
data_description.expose_carrier: Expose the detected shipping carrier when found in the email content.
data_description.expose_item_image: Expose a validated Amazon product image URL from the email content.
data_description.expose_parser_debug: Expose sanitized parser diagnostics. Raw text, addresses, order IDs, and full links are not included.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Amazon Orders
reconfigure.description: Update IMAP connection settings for this Amazon Order Status entry.

data_description.email: Email address of the mailbox that receives Amazon order emails.
data_description.password: Password or app password for IMAP login.
data_description.imap_port: IMAP SSL port. The default is 993.
data_description.imap_folder: The folder is stored through the integration options.

init.title: Amazon Order Options
init.description: Adjust scanning, processing, and visible sensor attributes.

scan.name: Scan and retention
scan.description: Controls how often and how far back the mailbox is scanned.

data_description.delivered_retention_days: Number of days delivered orders remain tracked in Home Assistant.
data_description.initial_scan_days: Number of days to scan back during the first scan or after empty storage.
data_description.imap_folder: Leave blank for INBOX or enter the exact folder name, for example INBOX/Amazon.

processing.name: Processing
processing.description: Controls which emails are processed and whether the mailbox is modified.

data_description.mark_as_read: Mark recognized Amazon status emails as read after processing.
data_description.require_amazon_sender: Ignore matching subjects unless sender headers use an Amazon domain.

attributes.name: Visible sensor attributes
attributes.description: These options control which potentially sensitive order details are available to dashboards.

data_description.expose_order_id: Expose Amazon order IDs in sensor attributes.
data_description.expose_item_title: Expose parsed item titles in sensor attributes.
data_description.expose_tracking_url: Expose validated HTTPS links to Amazon order or tracking pages.
data_description.expose_delivery_details: Expose delivery estimate, delivery window, delivered-at text, and item count from email content.
data_description.expose_carrier: Expose the detected shipping carrier when found in the email content.
data_description.expose_item_image: Expose a validated Amazon product image URL from the email content.
data_description.expose_parser_debug: Expose sanitized parser diagnostics. Raw text, addresses, order IDs, and full links are not included.

purge_order.name: Purge order
purge_order.description: Remove a specific order from tracking. The order will no longer appear in the Amazon Order Status sensors.

order_id.name: Order ID
order_id.description: The Amazon order number to remove, for example 123-4567890-1234567.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Optional Home Assistant config entry ID. Leave empty to purge from all loaded Amazon Order Status entries.

rescan.name: Rescan orders
rescan.description: Rescan Amazon order emails over a configurable lookback period.

days.name: Days
days.description: Number of days to scan back from now.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Optional Home Assistant config entry ID. Leave empty to rescan all loaded Amazon Order Status entries.

set_status.name: Set manual status
set_status.description: Set a manual status for one tracked order or one shipment within an order.

order_id.name: Order ID
order_id.description: The Amazon order number to update, for example 123-4567890-1234567.

status.name: Status
status.description: Manual order or shipment status to apply. Partially delivered is an order rollup status and is not valid with shipment_id.

shipment_id.name: Shipment ID
shipment_id.description: Optional shipment ID to update only one shipment from the order. Leave empty to update the whole order.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Optional Home Assistant config entry ID. Leave empty to update matching orders across all loaded Amazon Order Status entries.

mark_delivered.name: Mark delivered
mark_delivered.description: Mark one tracked order or one shipment as delivered manually.

order_id.name: Order ID
order_id.description: The Amazon order number to mark as delivered, for example 123-4567890-1234567.

shipment_id.name: Shipment ID
shipment_id.description: Optional shipment ID to mark only one shipment as delivered. Leave empty to update the whole order.

delivered_at.name: Delivered at
delivered_at.description: Optional ISO date/time to store with the manual delivery update, for example 2026-06-27 or 2026-06-27 18:30.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Optional Home Assistant config entry ID. Leave empty to update matching orders across all loaded Amazon Order Status entries.

ignore_order.name: Ignore order
ignore_order.description: Ignore one tracked order or one shipment so it no longer counts in active workflow sensors.

order_id.name: Order ID
order_id.description: The Amazon order number to ignore, for example 123-4567890-1234567.

shipment_id.name: Shipment ID
shipment_id.description: Optional shipment ID to ignore only one shipment. Leave empty to ignore the whole order.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Optional Home Assistant config entry ID. Leave empty to update matching orders across all loaded Amazon Order Status entries.

restore_order.name: Restore order
restore_order.description: Restore one ignored order or shipment so it returns to normal workflow tracking.

order_id.name: Order ID
order_id.description: The Amazon order number to restore, for example 123-4567890-1234567.

shipment_id.name: Shipment ID
shipment_id.description: Optional shipment ID to restore only one shipment. Leave empty to restore the whole order.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry ID
config_entry_id.description: Optional Home Assistant config entry ID. Leave empty to update matching orders across all loaded Amazon Order Status entries.
